title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
summary: The United States is preparing to build its first new aluminum smelter in more than three decades, with Century Aluminum planning a facility in Inola, Oklahoma. The plant could produce 500,000 tons of aluminum annually, including a portion designated for defense uses, and is intended to reduce reliance on imports.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Fox News reports that Century Aluminum is boosting production to full capacity amid tariff-related market shifts. The report focuses on how domestic aluminum production is responding to policy and demand pressures.

title: A Roadmap for Next Generation Supply Chain Resilience
summary: This white paper says the primary aluminum segment is operating at 53% of installed capacity and argues that rebuilding domestic aluminum production is necessary to meet growing demand. It outlines three main ways to expand U.S. capacity: new primary production, restarting or expanding existing plants, and increasing remelt capacity.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has reportedly told PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce its ore production quota this year. The move is intended to support global nickel prices, and the company says it will seek a revision.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: This market commentary says nickel prices have been supported by Indonesia's flat-to-down ore production targets for 2026. It argues that supply discipline in Indonesia, combined with rising EV demand, could push prices toward $20,000 per ton.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali, the Italian long stainless steel producer, signed an agreement to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, its stainless steel scrap supplier. The deal is intended to strengthen upstream integration and support the company’s circularity strategy. The transaction was expected to close in the coming months.

summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali completed the acquisition of a 65% stake in Com.steel Inox S.p.A. for €24 million. The company said the upstream integration will help it advance its circularity commitments and improve control over raw material supply.

title: Italy's Tenaris posts surprise 2% rise in sales on stable US, Canada drilling
summary: Tenaris reported a surprise 2% increase in third-quarter net sales, helped by steady drilling activity in North America that offset weakness in other regions. The company also warned that margins will be affected by the full impact of tariff costs.
